maturityBoundary: Baseline repository audit updated with exact-head worker process/image acceptance; risks remain open until immutable operational evidence closes them.

risks.finding: Four Vercel and one Deno failed contexts are still published on the exact main head.
risks.impact: Required checks and release evidence remain polluted by retired deployment authorities.
risks.mitigation: Remove integrations and verify a fresh control PR.
risks.acceptance: Combined status contains none of the five obsolete contexts.

risks.finding: A dedicated app_outbox process, non-root image and two-replica immutable-digest Helm workload now exist and pass exact-head process/image acceptance, but no production-like Kubernetes runtime evidence exists.
risks.impact: Independent scaling, rolling deployment, peer-pod failure and backlog recovery remain unproven in the target orchestration environment.
risks.mitigation: Deploy the exact worker image digest into production-like Kubernetes and execute pod kill, rolling deployment, backlog, telemetry and recovery drills.
risks.acceptance: Two live worker pods pass claim, graceful shutdown, peer kill, rolling deployment, lease, zero unintended duplicate effect and backlog recovery SLA with retained cluster evidence.

risks.finding: PostgreSQL is single-node in local and CI evidence; HA, pooler, WAL archive and PITR are unproven.
risks.impact: A database failure can violate availability and RTO/RPO targets.
risks.mitigation: Deploy HA PostgreSQL 16 with pooler and backup archive.
risks.acceptance: Measured RPO is at most 5 minutes and RTO at most 30 minutes with zero lost confirmed operations.

risks.finding: The worker workload requires an immutable OCI digest, but API and web Helm values still use mutable latest tags and no accepted cross-environment promotion manifest exists.
risks.impact: Build-once deploy-many and exact rollback cannot yet be proven for the complete release set.
risks.mitigation: Pin API and web OCI digests and produce promotion and rollback manifests for all deployable components.
risks.acceptance: The same API, web and worker image digests are promoted through environments and rollback restores the prior release without invariant failure.

risks.finding: Binary object immutability, quarantine, authorization and restore are not accepted.
risks.impact: Deal evidence can be unavailable, mutable or unsafe.
risks.mitigation: Enable versioning or object lock, quarantine and audited access.
risks.acceptance: Tamper, malware, unauthorized access and restore scenarios pass.

risks.finding: Full database plus object restore into a clean production-like environment is absent.
risks.impact: Recovery cannot be defended to a bank, regulator or enterprise buyer.
risks.mitigation: Run full backup, PITR and object reconciliation drill.
risks.acceptance: Clean restore meets RPO/RTO and all Deal, ledger, audit and evidence invariants.

risks.finding: Target 500 RPS, 5000 sessions, 50000 Deals and 10 million events are not measured.
risks.impact: Capacity and recovery backlog SLA are unknown.
risks.mitigation: Run Base, Target and Stress profiles.
risks.acceptance: All latency, error, invariant and backlog thresholds pass.

risks.finding: The infrastructure chaos matrix is incomplete.
risks.impact: Hidden corruption or prolonged outage may appear during process, network, storage or database failures.
risks.acceptance: Zero corruption and measured recovery with verified alerts and runbooks.

risks.finding: Blocking release scans exist, but the full production-like abuse matrix is incomplete.
risks.impact: Tenant escape, privilege escalation or unsafe object access may remain undiscovered.
risks.mitigation: Execute security acceptance against the deployed topology.
risks.acceptance: Critical and High findings are zero or covered by time-bounded formal exception; tenant leakage and privilege escalation are zero.

risks.finding: Local telemetry exists without accepted alert routing, ownership, escalation and SLO history.
risks.impact: Stuck Deals and infrastructure degradation may not be detected or acted on.
risks.mitigation: Deploy observability, define SLOs and verify every alert.
risks.acceptance: All required alerts fire, route and resolve through tested runbooks.

risks.finding: Required runbooks have not been independently executed as a complete set.
risks.impact: Recovery depends on hidden implementation knowledge.
risks.mitigation: Write and blind-test runbooks with a separate operator or isolated test agent.
risks.acceptance: runbook-validation.json passes every procedure without undocumented steps.

risks.finding: 72-hour, 14-day and 30-day soak evidence does not exist.
risks.impact: Repeatability and production operational acceptance cannot be claimed.
risks.mitigation: Run soak after all prerequisite gates pass.
risks.acceptance: 100 full-cycle Deals and required disputes, partials and recoveries complete with zero data loss, double settlement or tenant leakage.

risks.finding: Many stale and evidence-only pull requests remain open after their branches were superseded.
risks.impact: Authority, review and release state are harder to audit.
risks.mitigation: Classify and close stale pull requests without merging obsolete code.
risks.acceptance: Only active authority slices and explicitly retained evidence pull requests remain open.
